messages.content: Isaac is putting stickers in a sticker book. He put 1 sticker on the first page, 3 stickers on the second page, 9 stickers on the third page, and 27 stickers on the fourth page. What kind of sequence is this?
The given sequence is a geometric sequence.

Hayley is stacking cups; she put 26 plastic cups in the first stack, 21 plastic cups in the second stack, 16 plastic cups in the third stack, and 11 plastic cups in the fourth stack. What kind of sequence is this?
The given sequence is an arithmetic sequence.

What kind of sequence is this?

–53, –73, –93, –113, ...
The given sequence is an arithmetic sequence.
Bridget talks on the phone for 14 to 67 minutes each evening, depending on who calls. If you look at a log of her nightly telephone usage, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of Bridget's nightly telephone usage will be a discrete sequence, as it consists of distinct values (ranging from 14 to 67 minutes).
Bridget talks on the phone for 14 to 67 minutes each evening, depending on who calls. If you look at a log of her nightly telephone usage, what kind of sequence will you see? arithmetic or geometric
The sequence of Bridget's nightly telephone usage is not arithmetic or geometric. It is a discrete sequence that does not follow a specific pattern or equation.
Mark washes his hands 5 to 8 times each day, based on what he has been doing that day. If you make a list of the number of times he washes his hands daily, what kind of sequence will you have?

The sequence of the number of times Mark washes his hands daily will be a discrete sequence. It does not conform to an arithmetic or geometric progression since there is no consistent common difference or ratio.
Each year, Howell's Café makes 4 times as much money as the year before. If you list the annual earnings, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of Howell's Café's annual earnings will be a geometric sequence. The earnings are increasing by a constant ratio of 4 each year.
A growing amusement park is able to sell 7 times as many tickets each year as it did the year before. If you list the number of tickets sold over time, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of the number of tickets sold by the amusement park will be a geometric sequence. The number of tickets sold is increasing by a constant ratio of 7 each year.
Jared goes to the gym 7 times per week. If you track the total number of gym visits he has taken over time, what kind of sequence will you see?

The sequence of the total number of gym visits Jared has taken over time will be an arithmetic sequence. Each week, Jared goes to the gym 7 times, so the sequence will increase by a constant value of 7 each time.
Each day Maddie spends 31 minutes swimming. If you track Maddie's time spent swimming in total, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of Maddie's total time spent swimming will be an arithmetic sequence. Each day, Maddie spends 31 minutes swimming, so the sequence will increase by a constant value of 31 each time.

Each hour that a carton of milk is left out, there are 2 times as many bacteria as there were the hour before. If you list the number of bacteria in the milk each hour, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of the number of bacteria in the milk each hour will be a geometric sequence. The number of bacteria is increasing by a constant ratio of 2 each hour.
Pamela's hair is 2 centimetres longer each month than it was the month before. If you track her hair's length over time, what kind of sequence will you have?

The sequence of Pamela's hair length over time will be an arithmetic sequence. Each month, her hair grows 2 centimeters longer than the previous month, so the sequence will increase by a constant value of 2 each time.
is this arithmatic or geometric
The sequence of Pamela's hair length over time is an arithmetic sequence.

A child named Sophie is learning to read. Each year, Sophie is able to identify 4 times as many words as she was able to identify the year before. If you list the total number of words Sophie can identify each year, what kind of sequence will you see?
The sequence of the total number of words Sophie can identify each year will be a geometric sequence. Sophie's ability to identify words is increasing by a constant ratio of 4 each year.
is this geometric or arithmetic

Apologies for the incorrect response. The sequence of the total number of words Sophie can identify each year is actually a geometric sequence. Her ability to identify words is increasing by a constant ratio of 4 each year, which fits the definition of a geometric sequence.
